updates.title: Claude Cowork expands to web and mobile — tasks now run in the cloud with no device required
updates.summary: Anthropic announced on July 7 that Claude Cowork, previously desktop-only since its January launch, is now available on web and mobile for Max plan subscribers, with a gradual rollout to other plans. Tasks can now run autonomously in the cloud even when a user's device is closed, and Claude sends alerts when human input is needed. In a notable finding, Anthropic disclosed that more than 90% of Cowork usage is not software development — the top categories are business process operations (33.4%) and content creation/copywriting (16.4%). Chat and Cowork also now share a single unified home tab.
updates.accuracy_notes: Confirmed via Anthropic's official blog (claude.com/blog/cowork-web-mobile). Usage statistics (90%+ non-coding, 33.4% business ops, 16.4% content) cited directly from Anthropic data. Cross-verified by TechCrunch, VentureBeat, MacRumors, NBC News, 9to5Mac all reporting July 7, 2026. High confidence.

updates.title: Claude gains Microsoft 365 write tools — can now draft email, manage calendars, and create OneDrive/SharePoint files
updates.summary: Anthropic released write capabilities for the Microsoft 365 connector, significantly expanding beyond previous read-only search functionality. Claude can now draft and send emails, manage calendar events and mailbox settings, and create or update files in OneDrive and SharePoint — turning it into an active participant in the Microsoft 365 ecosystem rather than just a search tool.

updates.title: Claude for Government launches FedRAMP High beta — Claude Code and Cowork now available for public sector teams
updates.summary: Anthropic launched a public beta of Claude Code and Claude Cowork within Claude for Government Desktop, bringing FedRAMP High authorized AI to public sector teams for the first time. The offering includes desktop file-based work, stronger admin controls, tamper-evident audit logs, and spend governance tools designed for government agency requirements.

updates.title: Anthropic raises API rate limits and simplifies usage tiers to three levels: Start, Build, and Scale
updates.summary: Anthropic raised API rate limits for Claude Sonnet and Haiku models to match Claude Opus limits at every usage tier, and consolidated the usage tier structure into three levels: Start, Build, and Scale. The change makes it easier for developers to scale their applications without hitting model-specific rate limit ceilings and reduces friction in the developer onboarding and upgrade experience.
updates.accuracy_notes: Confirmed via Anthropic Developer Platform release notes aggregated for July 7, 2026. Specific token-per-minute figures for each new tier not yet confirmed in available search results. Verify exact limits and tier definitions at docs.anthropic.com.

updates.title: I used Claude Fable 5 for zero-shot coding, and understood why Anthropic locked it down
updates.summary: XDA reviews Claude Fable 5's coding performance after the model became temporarily accessible to paid subscribers (July 1-7) following its redeployment post-government export control suspension. The article explores Fable 5's exceptional zero-shot coding capabilities — and draws a direct line between that power and why the US Department of Commerce originally moved to restrict its global access on national security grounds. The article contextualizes July 7 as the final day Fable 5 is included in consumer plans before moving to API-only access via usage credits.
updates.accuracy_notes: Article and date confirmed via Google web search snippets showing July 7, 2026 publication. Timeline of events is accurate: Fable 5 launched June 9, US export control order June 12, redeployment July 1, plan inclusion ended July 7. Author's coding benchmarks are first-person/opinion — verify claims against official Anthropic benchmarks for Fable 5.
